owner: Provide clear instructions about your pet’s routine and preferences.
Ensure all necessary supplies are prepared before the service.
sitter: Review pet details carefully before starting the service.
Communicate with the owner if any issues arise during the service.

owner: Maintain a consistent feeding schedule to support digestion and behavior.
Provide a clean litter box daily to prevent health issues.
Ensure access to fresh water at all times.
Include interactive toys to prevent boredom and stress.
Schedule regular grooming to reduce shedding and hairballs.
sitter: Observe changes in appetite or litter usage as early health indicators.
Keep the environment calm and quiet, as cats are sensitive to stress.
Avoid sudden changes in routine or feeding type.
Ensure safe indoor space (no open windows, hazards).
Spend time building trust before handling the cat.

owner: Provide daily exercise to maintain physical and mental health.
Ensure a balanced diet based on age and breed.
Maintain regular vet check-ups and vaccinations.
Use basic training and commands for better behavior.
Keep the dog hydrated, especially in hot weather.
sitter: Follow the owner’s feeding and walking schedule strictly.
Monitor energy level and behavior changes.
Keep dogs on leash during outdoor activities.
Avoid introducing unfamiliar animals suddenly.
Provide attention and interaction to reduce anxiety.

owner: Provide a diet rich in hay and fresh vegetables.
Keep living area clean and well-ventilated.
Ensure rabbits have space for daily movement and exercise.
Regularly check teeth and fur condition.
Provide safe chew toys to prevent dental problems.
sitter: Handle rabbits gently to avoid stress or injury.
Ensure fresh food and water are always available.
Keep environment quiet and safe.
Monitor for signs of digestive issues (very common).
Avoid sudden loud noises or fast movements.

owner: Maintain clean water conditions with proper filtration.
Monitor water temperature and pH levels regularly.
Avoid overfeeding, which can pollute the tank.
Clean the tank periodically without disturbing fish too much.
Ensure fish are compatible species.
sitter: Feed fish in small, controlled portions.
Check if filtration system is working properly.
Avoid sudden changes in water temperature.
Observe for unusual behavior like floating or inactivity.
Do not add new fish without owner approval.

owner: Clean food and water dishes daily; remove spoiled soft foods promptly.
Offer a varied diet suited to the species; follow your vet’s guidance.
Provide safe flying or climbing space away from ceiling fans and toxins.
Schedule regular wing/nail/beak checks with an avian vet when needed.
Avoid non-stick cookware fumes and strong sprays near the bird’s area.
sitter: Note the owner’s feeding schedule and portion sizes for seed, pellets, and fresh food.
Cover water during misting or cleaning so droplets do not spoil dry feed.
Watch breathing, posture, and droppings; report anything unusual to the owner.
Secure doors and windows before any out-of-cage time.
Use calm movements and low voices; birds startle easily in new care settings.
